message:
test(frankenterm-core): add wa-p48pw LabRuntime deterministic tests for DirectMuxClient

Append a new labruntime_mux_client test submodule (feature-gated on
asupersync-runtime) with 8 deterministic tests exercising
DirectMuxClient channel primitives under the LabRuntime clock:

- pane_delta_channel_delivers_single_message: basic recv happy path
- pane_delta_try_send_backpressure: try_send returns Full when
  capacity exhausted, Ok when drained
- pane_delta_no_cross_talk: two concurrent channels deliver
  independently with no interleaving
- pane_delta_watch_cancellation_signal: shutdown watch propagates to
  pending recvs
- pane_delta_cancelled_cx_returns_error: cancelled Cx on
  pane_delta_recv_with_cx surfaces a cancellation error rather than
  hanging
- pane_delta_preserves_fifo_ordering: ordered send → ordered recv
  under LabRuntime virtual time
- pane_delta_reserve_commit_safety: reserve+commit semantics survive
  concurrent senders
- pane_delta_cancel_without_injection_is_safe: cancelling before any
  inject is a no-op

All additive (+342 lines in tests module); no runtime behavior
touched. Motivated by bead wa-p48pw (DirectMuxClient Cx-first
concurrency coverage).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
